title: Lankesteriana heloisae
intro: Lankesteriana heloisae, also known as the 'Queen of the Andes', is a type of orchid species native to the high-altitude regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lankesteriana heloisae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in various herbal preparations, including infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Lankesteriana heloisae is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of epiphytic orchid that grows in the wild. Lankesteriana heloisae is native to Central America, specifically in countries such as Costa Rica and Panama. The classification of this orchid species is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Subfamily: Catasetoideae, Tribe: Cataseteae, Genus: Lankesteriana, Species: L. heloisae.
